commit.message:
cfl: regenerate patches 079-081, delete 6 NO-OPs, harden build.sh

Regenerated 3 malformed patches that never applied:
- CFL-079: ApplySequence runtime depth limit (16) — CWE-674
- CFL-080: XYZ Describe output size cap (1MB) — CWE-400
- CFL-081: DescribeSequence recursion depth limit (32) — CWE-674

CFL-079 now includes both .h declaration changes (ApplySequence +
DescribeSequence signatures) and .cpp ApplySequence implementation.
CFL-081 has only .cpp DescribeSequence hunks (header handled by 079).

Deleted 6 NO-OP patches (upstreamed or superseded):
- 006 (upstreamed), 043 (superseded by 047), 048 (superseded by 074)
- 064 (upstreamed PR #652), 070 (upstreamed PR #654), 072 (upstreamed PR #657)

Hardened build.sh patch loop:
- Distinguish 'already applied' (reverse dry-run) from 'FAIL' (malformed)
- Report summary: N applied, N skipped, N failed
- Warn when failures detected (CWE-400 protections may be missing)

Verified:
- 62 patches, 0 failures, 18 fuzzers built
- All 8 timeout artifacts complete instantly (<100ms)
- 230/230 unit tests pass
- Fuzzers synced to SSD at /mnt/g/fuzz-ssd/bin/

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(types): make templateParams extensible via module augmentation (#679)

* fix(types): make templateParams extensible via module augmentation

Add `templateParams` to `SchemaAugmentations` and intersect it into
`ResolvableTemplateParams`, allowing users to extend templateParams
via `declare module 'unhead/types' { interface SchemaAugmentations { templateParams: { foo: string } } }`.
Also updates `ReactiveHead.templateParams` in the Vue package to use
the shared `ResolvableTemplateParams` type.

Closes #575

* fix(types): make templateParams extensible via module augmentation (#684)

* Initial plan

* fix(types): use dedicated TemplateParamsAugmentations interface to avoid TS2717

commit.message:
feat: retry partial collections after rate-limit cool-down

Add a second sequential pass for partial collections after
the main concurrent sweep. When Fragment rate-limits specific
collection endpoints during the parallel phase (concurrency=4),
those collections are retried one-by-one after a 15s cool-down
(SCRAPER_PARTIAL_RETRY_DELAY_SECONDS) using fresh sessions.

This eliminates the remaining ~70 partial for_sale collections
without requiring proxy rotation or lower overall concurrency.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(config): allow invalid APNS key when APNS_ENABLED=false

Tests and dev environments may have APNS_DEVICE_TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY
set to a placeholder that is not valid 64 hex chars. When APNS is
disabled, this is harmless — use zero placeholder instead of bailing.
Only fail when APNS_ENABLED=true with missing or invalid key.

* feat: enhance LEI related-entity UX and release v0.3.0

* fix(lei-records): refine successor loading and throttle lookups

* Remove truncation from currency_codes and languages cells in Countries table (#152)

* fix: remove truncation from currency_codes and languages table cells

Replace the `max-w-[18rem] overflow-hidden whitespace-nowrap text-ellipsis`
wrapper div with `whitespace-normal break-words` on the td directly.
This allows full content to wrap and be visible per the UI guidelines
that discourage truncating potentially-long table values without an
always-visible expand affordance.

The redundant outer `title` attribute is also removed since
ReferenceDetailList already provides per-item detail tooltips.

refactor: split monolithic index.html into modular CSS/JS architecture

- css/variables.css: CSS custom properties and reset
- css/animations.css: all @keyframe animations
- css/components.css: button, input, modal, list, AI badge styles
- css/main.css: body, layout, section, map, location display styles
- js/config.js: application constants (CONFIG object)
- js/neural-network.js: NeuralNetwork class with reset() method
- js/database.js: IndexedDB wrapper (Database class)
- js/utils.js: pure utility functions (escapeHtml, geocoding, smoothing, etc.)
- js/gps.js: GPSManager class (location, tracking, AI data collection)
- js/map.js: MapManager class (Leaflet map, markers, tile layers)
- js/ui.js: UIManager class (event listeners, modals, points list, AI stats)
- js/app.js: GPSNavigatorApp root class wiring all modules together
- index.html: remove all inline CSS, inline JS, onclick attributes;
  update CSP to remove 'unsafe-inline'; link external CSS/JS files

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: replace getUser() with local JWT decode in edge function

- Remove supabase.auth.getUser(token) which was making a live HTTP call
  to Supabase Auth and failing with 'JWT is not valid'
- The Supabase gateway already validates the JWT signature before the
  function runs, so we decode the payload locally (atob) to get sub
- Fix list_users: reassign query variable (chained .eq() was not applied)
- Fix profile lookups: .single() -> .maybeSingle() to handle missing rows
- Add delete_user action, update_clinic, list_clinics actions
- Add rollback logic in create_user (delete auth user if profile/role fails)
- Clean up debug console.log statements

commit.message:
fix(scripts): use AzureCliCredential in CI for generate.env.ts

In CI, azure/login authenticates as the Infrastructure UAMI via OIDC and
sets up the Azure CLI context. But AZURE_CLIENT_ID is set to the Frontend
UAMI, causing DefaultAzureCredential to try ManagedIdentityCredential
with the wrong identity (which doesn't exist in CI anyway).

Use AzureCliCredential directly in CI (detected via CI/GITHUB_ACTIONS env)
so the token is acquired through the azure/login OIDC session, which uses
the Infrastructure UAMI's federated credential.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
cfl: fix patch path format for CFL-074, 075, 076 (CWE-400 timeout)

Three CWE-400 timeout patches (074, 075, 076) used plain paths instead of
git a/b/ format. build.sh uses `patch -p1` which strips the first path
component — with plain paths like `IccProfLib/X`, -p1 stripped the
directory prefix, causing all 3 patches to silently SKIP on every build.

Result: CheckUnderflowOverflow had depth=100 (should be 16) and no ops
budget, EvaluateProfile had no iteration cap, NamedColor2 had no nCoords
cap. All documented timeout protections were never active.

Fix: Convert all 3 patches to git `--- a/` / `+++ b/` format.
Verified: all 3 apply cleanly with -p1. Timeout artifacts that took 300+s
now complete in <30ms. 18/18 fuzzers rebuilt. 230/230 analyzer tests pass.

Co-authored-by: XSFORM <216679127+XSFORM@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(runtimed): kill kernel processes on daemon shutdown (#664)

* fix(runtimed): kill kernel processes on daemon shutdown

Kernels are spawned with process_group(0) so they don't receive the
SIGINT/SIGTERM that hits the daemon. Previously the daemon relied on
RoomKernel's Drop impl to killpg the process groups, but Drop doesn't
reliably fire because:

1. RoomKernel is behind Arc<Mutex<Option<...>>> inside Arc<NotebookRoom>
   and multiple spawned tasks hold Arc clones that may not all unwind
   during tokio runtime teardown.
2. A second ctrl-c or SIGKILL skips destructors entirely.

Now we explicitly drain all notebook rooms and call kernel.shutdown()
(which sends ShutdownRequest + SIGKILL to the process group and removes
the connection file) before run() returns.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01FDgQx2NBjRgtUxgcfqEt43

* Update daemon.rs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(mvsa): Node0 MVSA sign-off — authority resolution, Rust proof, lifecycle v2

Wave 1 — Authority Closure:
- core/sovereign/node0_authority.py: 4-level precedence chain
  (canonical→legacy_ceremony→legacy_reference→fail-closed)
- core/sovereign/atomic_io.py: shared write→fsync→rename utility
- activate() now derives identity from canonical genesis, not onboarding
- PAT/SAT rosters come exclusively from authority artifacts

Wave 2 — Rust MVSA Proof:
- bizra-omega/bizra-resourcepool/src/bin/node0_mvsa.rs: validates genesis,
  binds loopback, runs BLAKE3 self-validation, emits JSON proof artifact
- core/sovereign/node0_mvsa.py: Python wrapper with 5-step binary resolution
- prove-mvsa CLI subcommand + GET /mvsa + POST /prove-mvsa API routes

Wave 3 — Lifecycle v2:
- Schema 2.0.0 with 11 gates (genesis_authority_valid through restart_recovery_ready)
- Status: blocked→degraded→ready (exit codes: 3/2/0)
- health() reads v1 or v2 without crashing
- task updates mission_path_receipted; health validates restart_recovery

Tests: 93/93 pass (8 authority + 7 MVSA + 7 origin_guard + 8 standalone + 30 mission + 33 organism)

commit.message:
fix: run price_sync listing statuses sequentially to avoid rate limiting

Parallel for_sale+auction collection scans hit Fragment rate limits,
causing 82/109 collections to return 0 items (empty_collection_retries).
Sequential execution eliminates double-concurrency pressure while keeping
the ~70x speedup over detail-based price_sync.

commit.message:
fix: include base_os_name/version in upsert ON CONFLICT UPDATE (#19)

* fix: include base_os_name/version in upsert ON CONFLICT UPDATE

InsertImage() ON CONFLICT clause was missing base_os_name and
base_os_version, so re-scanning existing images never updated
the OS fields. This caused all images to show 'Other' in reports
even after OS extraction from Trivy metadata was added.

Add TestUpsertImage_UpdatesBaseOS to verify the fix.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Clarify skillpm’s position relative to GitHub CLI/Claude plugin specs (#57)

Users asked what emerging GitHub CLI and Claude plugin specs imply for
skillpm. This PR clarifies that those specs are host/runtime extension
mechanisms, while skillpm remains focused on portable Agent Skills
packaging and orchestration.

- **Documentation: plugin-spec positioning**
  - Added a new section to `README.md`:
    - `How this relates to plugin specs (GitHub CLI / Claude Code)`
  - Added a matching section to `docs/index.md`:
    - `How this relates to plugin specs`
  - Both sections explicitly state:
    - plugin specs and Agent Skills are complementary, not replacements
- skillpm’s model is unchanged (`npm` for deps + `skills` for wiring +
`add-mcp` for MCP config)

- **Docs consistency**
- Kept language aligned across repository README and docs homepage so
users get the same answer regardless of entry point.

Example excerpt added:

```md
- Plugin specs define host-specific extension/runtime hooks.
- Agent Skills define portable instruction packages that can be reused across agent systems.

For skillpm, the model stays the same: npm for dependency management, plus `skills` and `add-mcp` for wiring and MCP configuration.
```

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT SUFFIX -->



<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> I read about a plugin spec for GitHub cli and Claude code. What does
that mean for out project?


</details>



<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT TIPS -->
---

✨ Let Copilot coding agent [set things up for
you](https://github.com/sbroenne/skillpm/issues/new?title=✨+Set+up+Copilot+instructions&body=Configure%20instructions%20for%20this%20repository%20as%20documented%20in%20%5BBest%20practices%20for%20Copilot%20coding%20agent%20in%20your%20repository%5D%28https://gh.io/copilot-coding-agent-tips%29%2E%0A%0A%3COnboard%20this%20repo%3E&assignees=copilot)
— coding agent works faster and does higher quality work when set up for

commit.message:
fix: recompile daily-testify-uber-super-expert with gh-aw v0.57.0

The v0.45.2 compiler generated abstract runtime-import references
(github/gh-aw/.github/workflows/shared/mood.md@sha) that the runtime
resolver could not find. The v0.57.0 compiler generates correct paths
pointing to .github/aw/imports/ where the cached files actually live.

Also adds actions-lock.json and agentics-maintenance.yml generated
by the newer compiler version.

commit.message:
fix: handle Whisper 25MB file size limit with auto-chunking

- Lower audio bitrate 64k -> 32k (halves file size, supports ~2h videos)
- Add _split_audio(): auto-splits oversized files via FFmpeg into chunks
- Add _transcribe_single(): transcribe one chunk at a time
- transcribe() now handles large files transparently: split -> transcribe each -> merge
- Chunk temp dirs are cleaned up after transcription
- Add 3 new tests: small file (no split), large file splitting, chunk merge

commit.message:
feat(layout): propagate SetTheme to all Themeable children

Previously Panel.SetTheme only propagated themes to child panels,
ignoring Button, Label, TextInput, and ScrollView widgets.

This commit:
- Defines a Themeable interface in publicwidget.go
- Updates Panel.SetTheme to use type assertion for Themeable
- All concrete widgets (Button, Label, TextInput, ScrollView, Panel)
  now receive theme updates when set on parent containers
- Adds tests for theme propagation and Themeable interface

Fixes PLAN.md item 1d.

commit.message:
Bytt fra ParallelGC til ZGC og øk heap fra 25% til 50%

Appen bruker mesteparten av tiden på å deserialisere og forkaste store JSON-meldinger
fra det delte rapids-topicet (teamdagpenger.rapid.v1). Kun en liten andel av meldingene
matcher PdfBehov/SaksbehandlingPdfBehov — resten filtreres bort etter full JSON-parsing.

Dette skaper et mønster med konstant allokering og forkasting av store, kortlevde objekter,
som er det verste scenarioet for ParallelGC (full stop-the-world-pauser). Vi observerer at
ytelsen degraderer over tid: fra 1 sek til 3+ sek per batch etter ~15 minutter, forårsaket
av stadig hyppigere og lengre GC-pauser. Podden som eier den travleste partisjonen bruker
opptil 1000m CPU og klarer ikke holde tritt med meldingsraten.

Endringer:
- ParallelGC -> ZGC: Sub-millisekund GC-pauser uavhengig av heap-størrelse.
  ZGC gjør mesteparten av arbeidet concurrent, uten å stoppe applikasjonstråder.
- MaxRAMPercentage 25% -> 50%: Gir 3072Mi heap i stedet for 1536Mi. Mer headroom
  reduserer GC-frekvensen og gir bedre gjennomstrømning for JSON-deserialisering.
- Fjernet JMX remote-flagg: Ubrukt i produksjon, frigjør ressurser og lukker port 9999.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(infra): add Monitoring Metrics Publisher RBAC for App Insights

Azure Monitor OTel exporters using token-based auth (Managed Identity)
require the Monitoring Metrics Publisher role to push traces, metrics,
and logs to Application Insights. Without it, all telemetry exports
fail with 403 Forbidden.

Changes:
- Add monitoringMetricsPublisher GUID to constants/roles.bicep
- Create rbac/app-insights-rbac.bicep assigning the role to all 3 UAMIs
  (frontend, backend, infrastructure) scoped to the App Insights resource
- Wire into facade.bicep as appInsightsRbac module
- Export appInsightsName from observability deployment for RBAC scoping

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
